channel.title: Bloomberg Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: Humanoid Robots Still Lack AI Technology, Unitree CEO Says
item.description: Artificial intelligence technology to get humanoid robots into the mainstream remains a key challenge for the sector, according to the founder of one of China’s prominent robot developers.
item.dc:creator: John Liu, Jessica Sui

media:content.media:description: A Unitree Robotics humanoid robot during the Super AI Conference in Singapore, on Wednesday, June 18, 2025. The conference runs through June 19.

item.title: AI Chip Firm DeepX Hires Morgan Stanley for Funding Before IPO
item.description: South Korean AI chip designer DeepX Co. has hired Morgan Stanley to assist with a round of capital-raising ahead of a potential initial public offering in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Yoolim Lee

media:content.media:description: LAS VEGAS, NEVADA - JANUARY 10: A view of DEEPX booth at CES 2024, the world's largest annual consumer technology trade on January 10, 2024 at the Las Vegas Convention Center in Las Vegas, Nevada, United States. (Photo by Tayfun Coskun/Anadolu via Getty Images)

item.title: C3.ai Reports Sales Far Short of Estimates, Shakes Up Management
item.description: C3.ai Inc. released preliminary first-quarter revenue that fell far short of analysts’ estimates and restructured its sales management team. The shares fell about 10% in extended trading.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Pollack

media:content.media:description: DAVOS, SWITZERLAND - JANUARY 21: C3.ai store is seen at Promenade Street in Davos, Switzerland on January 21, 2025. The world's leading companies temporarily took over the cafes, shops and restaurants on the Promenade street and organized them as meeting and event spaces. (Photo by Ömer Sercan Karku/Anadolu via Getty Images)

item.title: Ex-OpenAI, DeepMind Staffers Set for $1 Billion Value in Andreessen-Led Round
item.description: Venture firm Andreessen Horowitz has agreed to lead a $200 million investment in Periodic Labs, a new startup building artificial intelligence for material science, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Kate Clark

media:content.media:description: A symbol for the OpenAI virtual assistant on a smartphone, arranged in Riga, Latvia, on Friday, Aug. 16, 2024. The public release of advanced generative AI tools such as Google’s Gemini, Meta AI, and OpenAI’s ChatGPT over the past two years has heightened fears that millions of workers could be displaced.

item.title: Stablecoin Growth is Hampered by Nagging Foreign Exchange Costs
item.description: Even as stablecoins enter a period of peak expectations, veterans in wider fintech circles see limitations to the tokens as an emerging payments tool.
item.dc:creator: Sidhartha Shukla

item.title: Meta’s $29 Billion Deal Marks Key Moment for Private Credit
item.description: The heavy hitters of private credit have been waiting for this moment for years.
item.dc:creator: Carmen Arroyo, Olivia Fishlow

media:content.media:description: A Meta store in Burlingame, California, US, on Friday, Oct. 11, 2024. Meta Platforms Inc. is scheduled to release earnings figures on October 30. Photographer David Paul Morris/Bloomberg

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5 Met With Mixed Reviews, Confusion in First Day
item.description: For months, OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman has been hyping up the capabilities of GPT-5, setting up the launch as a seminal moment for the company. But in the first 24 hours after its release, the new model was met with mixed reviews.
item.dc:creator: Emily Forgash

media:content.media:description: Sam Altman, chief executive officer of OpenAI Inc., speaks during the Federal Reserve Integrated Review of the Capital Framework for Large Banks Conference in Washington, DC, US, on Tuesday, July 22, 2025. The conference is meant to provide expert perspectives on the key pillars of the regulatory capital framework.

item.title: OpenAI’s COO Lightcap Talks GPT-5 Launch | Bloomberg Tech 8/8/2025
item.description: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde speaks with OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap about the company’s latest model, GPT-5, and the battle to retain top talent in the artificial intelligence space. Plus, SoftBank and Meta each make moves to expand their AI data center resources. And the CEOs of Twilio, Chime, and Akamai each discuss their companies’ latest earnings reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Trade Desk Sentiment Collapses as Specter of Amazon Looms
item.description: Trade Desk Inc. analysts are bailing on the one-time market favorite amid growing fears that its advertising technology will get steamrolled by Amazon.com Inc.’s offerings.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Vlastelica

item.title: Figma’s $21 Billion Drop Returns Stock to Earth After IPO Frenzy
item.description: Figma Inc. has taken investors who pounced on the year’s hottest initial public offering for a wild ride, shedding $21 billion from a peak in the days following its record-breaking IPO.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Lipschultz

media:content.media:description: Figma Inc. signage during the company's initial public offering (IPO) at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in New York, US, on Thursday, July 31, 2025. Figma Inc. is expected to price its US initial public offering at $33 per share, according to people familiar with the matter, above the design and collaboration software company's increased price range.

item.title: James Lovell, Space-Age Hero Who Commanded Apollo 13, Dies at 97
item.description: James Lovell, the pioneering US astronaut whose two dramatic missions to the moon included Apollo 13, the nearly disastrous trip that captivated the world and decades later inspired a triumphant Hollywood blockbuster, has died. He was 97.
item.dc:creator: Laurence Arnold

media:content.media:description: Jim Lovell

item.title: Ex-Blizzard Developers Bet It All on ‘Stormgate’ Redemption Story
item.description: Frost Giant Studios set out to make a successor to StarCraft II, but the road has been difficult so far
item.dc:creator: Jason Schreier

media:content.media:description: Stormgate leaves Early Access this week with the launch of a full campaign

item.title: Latest US Court System Hack Is a Throwback to 2020 SolarWinds
item.dc:creator: Jeff Stone

media:content.media:description: The Mark O. Hatfield United States Courthouse in Portland, Oregon, US, on Thursday, Sept. 5, 2024. Kroger Co. and Albertsons Cos. supermarkets say they need to combine to compete against bigger rivals Amazon Inc., Costco Wholesale Corp. and Walmart Inc., while the Federal Trade Commission alleges the tie-up would lead to higher grocery prices for consumers and lower wages for the supermarkets' unionized workforces. Photographer: Elayna Yussen/Bloomberg

item.title: Columbia Hack Affected 870,000 People, Included Some Health Data
item.description: A recent breach of Columbia University’s computer systems compromised personal information of about 870,000 people, including students and applicants, according to the school’s reports to state officials in the US.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Fozi

media:content.media:description: "Columbia University New York City, please see also my other images of New York in my lightbox:" Photographer: Lya_Cattel/E+

item.title: Sezzle Is Up 7,000% Since Bottoming in 2023. The Question Is Why
item.description: Usually when a stock soars more than 7,000% in less than two years, investors have some idea why. Then there’s the curious case of Sezzle Inc.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Bradin

media:content.media:description: Sezzle, the buy now, pay later firm app

item.title: SoftBank Buys Foxconn’s Ohio Plant to Boost Stargate AI Push
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is the buyer taking ownership of Foxconn Technology Group’s electric vehicle plant in Ohio, a move aimed at kick-starting the Japanese company’s $500 billion Stargate data center project with OpenAI and Oracle Corp.
item.dc:creator: Edward Ludlow, Min Jeong Lee, Debby Wu

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump stands with Foxconn founder Terry Gou and SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son in 2018.

item.title: Tesla Disbands Dojo Supercomputer Team, Unwinding Key AI Effort
item.description: Tesla Inc. is disbanding its Dojo team and its leader will leave the company, according to people familiar with the matter, upending the automaker’s effort to build an in-house supercomputer for developing driverless-vehicle technology.
item.dc:creator: Edward Ludlow

media:content.media:description: New Tesla Inc. vehicles in a parking lot after arriving at a port at dusk in Yokohama, Japan, on Sunday, July 13, 2025. Japan will release its trade figures on July 17.

item.title: Intel Investors Suffer Fresh Setback as CEO in Trump Crosshairs
item.description: Intel Corp. investors can’t catch a break.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Vlastelica

media:content.media:description: Lip-Bu Tan, chief executive officer of Intel Corp., during a news conference on the sidelines of the Computex conference in Taipei, Taiwan, on Monday, May 19, 2025. Tan is currently weighing options to reform the storied US chipmaker.

item.title: Meta Picks Pimco, Blue Owl for $29 Billion Data Center Deal
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has selected Pacific Investment Management Co. and Blue Owl Capital Inc. to lead a $29 billion financing for its data center expansion in rural Louisiana as the race for artificial intelligence infrastructure heats up, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Carmen Arroyo, Laura Benitez, Kurt Wagner

media:content.media:description: Meta store. Photographer: David Paul Morris/Bloomberg

item.title: Money Is Raining Down on Green-Tech Firms That Have an AI Story
item.description: Long-unloved nuclear and geothermal are experiencing a boom in interest as data center developers seek carbon-free resources capable of delivering round-the-clock electricity to power artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Ma, Mark Chediak

media:content.media:description: An Amazon Web Services data center in Manassas, Virginia.

item.title: A $25 Billion Crypto-Hoarding Craze Is Testing Market Nerves
item.description: The rush to convert listed companies into vehicles for buying cryptocurrencies has reached such a fever pitch that even executives backing such deals are warning of potential fallout for digital-asset prices.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Weeks

item.title: Wall Street and AI Startups Are Fighting Over Entry-Level Quants
item.description: At a rooftop bar on Manhattan’s Lower East Side, roughly 150 quant researchers met with employees at the artificial-intelligence startup Anthropic who implored them to consider a life away from Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Brunella Tipismana Urbano, Anika Arora Seth

media:content.media:description: Signage of AI (Artificial Intelligence) is seen during the World Audio Visual Entertainment Summit in Mumbai, India, on May 02, 2025. (Photo by Indranil Aditya / Middle East Images / Middle East Images via AFP) (Photo by INDRANIL ADITYA/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images) Photographer: INDRANIL ADITYA/AFP

item.title: Congo Peace May Herald $700 Million Power Deal With US Company
item.description: Symbion Power LLC is proposing a 140-megawatt power plant and transmission lines along the border of Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda if the two countries can implement a recent US-backed peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Michael J Kavanagh

media:content.media:description: Technicians repair electrical transmission lines following power outages caused by clashes as residents begin to venture onto the streets in Goma on January 31, 2025. The Rwandan-backed armed group M23 moved south as it closed in on a key military airport in DR Congo on January 31, 2025, a day after pledging to take the capital Kinshasa and as international criticism mounted. The group's capture of most of Goma, the capital of North Kivu province, earlier in the week was a dramatic escalation in a region that has seen decades of conflict involving multiple armed groups. (Photo by Jospin Mwisha / AFP) (Photo by JOSPIN MWISHA/AFP via Getty Images) Photographer: JOSPIN MWISHA/AFP

item.title: Uber Walks Fine Line to Satisfy Shareholders on Driverless Car Investments
item.description: Welcome to Tech In Depth, our daily newsletter about the business of tech from Bloomberg’s journalists around the world. Today, Natalie Lung reports on Uber’s strategy to invest in the future of robotaxis.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Lung

media:content.media:description: Uber Technologies Inc. is teaming up with Lucid Group Inc. and self-driving startup Nuro to launch a robotaxi fleet.

item.title: Turkey to Block Veteran Journalist’s YouTube Channel After Arrest
item.description: A Turkish court ordered the removal of all content from the popular YouTube channel of veteran journalist Fatih Altayli, following his arrest on charges of threatening President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
item.dc:creator: Taylan Bilgic

media:content.media:description: The logo for YouTube application arranged on a smartphone in Hong Kong, China, on Wednesday, March 6, 2024. The Hong Kong government’s document summarizing recent public consultations on proposed domestic security legislation included suggestions that some popular messaging apps be prohibited, though authorities stopped short of endorsing that view. Photographer: Lam Yik/Bloomberg

item.title: Taiwan Exceeds Full-Year Record for Trade Surplus With US
item.description: Taiwan’s trade surplus with the US exceeded its full-year record in just seven months, as a global boom in artificial intelligence fuels demand for its tech products.
item.dc:creator: Yian Lee, Miaojung Lin

item.title: Tesla Disbands Dojo Supercomputer Team, Upending AI Work
item.description: Tesla Inc. is disbanding its Dojo team, upending the automaker’s effort to build an in-house supercomputer for developing driverless-vehicle technology. The team has lost about 20 workers recently to newly formed DensityAI, and remaining Dojo workers are being reassigned to other data center and compute projects within Tesla. Craig Trudell reports on Bloomberg Television.

item.title: What’s the De Minimis Tariff Loophole and Why Is Trump Closing It?
item.description: A Latin term that used to be little-known outside the world of customs brokers has become the stuff of headlines this year. That’s thanks to a decision by US President Donald Trump to close a tariff loophole for “de minimis” merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Murray, Samson Ellis

media:content.media:description: The Temu app on a smartphone in Shanghai, China, on Feb. 3, 2025.

item.title: Emirates Expands Electronics Restrictions With Power Bank Ban
item.description: Emirates will ban the use of power banks aboard its flights and only allow passengers to bring one such device onto the plane, further restricting travelers from carrying electronic gadgets on aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Leen Al-Rashdan

media:content.media:description: An Airbus SE A380-800 Emirates plane sits at Toronto Pearson International Airport (YYZ) in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on Monday, July 22, 2019. In 2018, 49.5 million passengers traveled through Pearson on 473,000 flights.

item.title: European Tech Stocks Miss Out on AI Frenzy as Earnings Falter
item.description: European technology stocks are woefully underperforming their US peers over the past six months, and their much weaker earnings momentum is to blame.
item.dc:creator: Henry Ren
